inputs.corpus: Across 41 tool-use observations the agent repeatedly issued file reads and edits with bare relative paths from a working directory that changed between Bash calls, producing 7 file-not-found failures and 3 wrong-file edits. In every recovery the agent re-issued the same operation with an absolute path and it succeeded on the first retry.
inputs.existing_rules_summary: 1 active rule: 'prefer-structured-logging' (tooling, confirmed). No rule governs path style for file operations.

rule_under_test.name: prefer-absolute-paths-in-file-ops
rule_under_test.content: Always use absolute paths for file read, write, and edit operations. The working directory can change between commands, so relative paths are unreliable.

expected_judgment.rationale: The corpus shows a direct, repeated failure mode (relative paths against a shifting CWD) that the rule eliminates; every observed recovery already applied exactly this fix. Strong, well-grounded positive.
